extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Only two rules affect A and B: rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50) sets rec_drug_a:=true when (g6pd_deficiency AND seizure_history AND heart_failure AND hepatic_impairment); rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) sets rec_drug_b:=true when g6pd_deficiency. The trigger condition for A is a strict superset of the condition for B, since both require g6pd_deficiency=true. Thus any input that fires the A rule necessarily also fires the B rule. With g6pd_deficiency=seizure_history=heart_failure=hepatic_impairment=true, rec_a_from_conjunction fires (rec_drug_a:=true, the sole rule for A, no conflicting rule to override) and rec_b_from_trigger fires (rec_drug_b:=true, the sole rule for B). Defaults apply only to outputs left unset, so neither A nor B reverts to false. Both outputs are true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. The other rules only set rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; bradycardia, immunosuppressed, and anticoagulated are set false for a minimal witness.
